svg.text: Thundering herd problem when a hot cache key expires under high concurrency
Stampede Formation: The Thundering Herd Problem
On expired read during SWR window:
Zero blocking. First request after expiry gets stale data,
triggers one background refresh. All concurrent reads get stale.
Expiration probability increases as TTL nears end:
When a request arrives and TTL is >80% elapsed,
flip a biased coin. If heads: treat as miss, refresh.
Spreads refresh load over time instead of cliff.
No locking, no stale data, probabilistic smoothing
Latency Impact: With vs Without Stampede Protection
- All N requests hit DB simultaneously
- Others served stale or wait briefly
